{{Short description|Men's national football team representing Albania}}
{{About|the men's team|the women's team|Albania women's national football team}}
{{Use British English|date=July 2024}}
{{Use dmy dates|date=November 2023}}

| Confederation      = [[UEFA]] (Europe)
| Coach              = [[Rolando Maran]]
| Captain            = [[Berat Djimsiti|Berat Gjimshiti]]
| Most caps          = [[Elseid Hysaj]] (100)
| Top scorer         = [[Erjon Bogdani]] (18)
| Home Stadium       = [[Arena Kombëtare]]

| Largest win        = {{fb|ALB}} 5–0 {{fb-rt|VIE}}<br />([[Bastia Umbra]], Italy; 12 February 2003)<br />{{fb|ALB}} 6–1 {{fb-rt|CYP}}<br />([[Tirana]], Albania; 12 August 2009)<br />{{fb|ALB}} 5–0 {{fb-rt|SMR}}<br />([[Elbasan]], Albania; 8 September 2021)
| Largest loss       = {{fb|HUN|1949}} 12–0 {{fb-rt|ALB|1946}}<br />([[Budapest]], Hungary; 24 September 1950)

| World cup best     =
| Regional name      = [[UEFA European Championship|European Championship]]

| Regional cup first = [[UEFA Euro 2016|2016]]
| Regional cup best  = Group stage ([[UEFA Euro 2016|2016]], [[UEFA Euro 2024|2024]])
| medaltemplates     = {{MedalSport|Men's [[Association football|football]]}}
{{Medal|Competition|[[Balkan Cup]]}}
{{MedalGold|[[1946 Balkan Cup|1946 Albania]]|[[1946 Balkan Cup#Albania|Team]]}}
{{Medal|Competition|[[Malta International Football Tournament|Malta "Rothmans" International Tournament]]}}
{{MedalGold|[[2000 Malta International Football Tournament|2000 Malta]]|[[2000 Malta International Football Tournament#Albania|Team]]}}
{{MedalBronze|[[1998 Malta International Football Tournament|1998 Malta]]|[[2000 Malta International Football Tournament#Albania|Team]]}}
}}

The '''Albania national football team''' ({{langx|sq|Kombëtarja shqiptare e futbollit}}) represents [[Albania]] in men's international [[Association football|football]]. It is governed by the [[Albanian Football Federation]] (FSHF), the governing body for [[football in Albania]]. It is a member of [[UEFA]] in Europe and [[FIFA]] in global competitions. The team's colours reference two national symbols: the [[double-headed eagle]] and the [[Flag of Albania|country's bicolor]]. Their supporters are colloquially referred to as the ''[[Tifozat Kuq e Zi]]'' (Red and Black Fans).

Since [[History of the Albania national football team|its inception]] as a team, Albania first collected a major tournament trophy in [[1946 Balkan Cup|1946]] with the [[Balkan Cup]], followed by the [[Malta International Football Tournament|Malta International Tournament]] ([[2000 Malta International Football Tournament|2000]]). At the [[FIFA World Cup]], Albania has campaigned to qualify since their entry into FIFA in 1932. The team has reached the group stage of [[UEFA European Championship]] twice ([[UEFA Euro 2016|2016]], [[UEFA Euro 2024|2024]]). They have competed in the [[UEFA Nations League]] since 2018.

Upon its admission into FIFA ranked 124th, Albania ascended to their peak [[FIFA Men's World Ranking|World Ranking]] of 22nd in 2015. The country plays their home games in the [[Arena Kombëtare]] in their capital city, [[Tirana]].

==History==
{{main|History of the Albania national football team}}

===20th century===
[[File:Loro Boriçi në finalen e Ballkaniadës.jpg|160px|thumb|[[Loro Boriçi]] captained the national team in winning the [[1946 Balkan Cup]].]]
Although it never played any matches, the Albania national football team existed before the [[Albanian Football Association]] (Federata Shqiptare e Futbollit; FSHF) was created on 6 June 1930. Albania joined [[FIFA]] during a congress held between 12 and 16 June. Albania was invited to play in the 1934 World Cup, but did not take part due to logistical problems.<ref>{{cite web | url=http://www.giovanniarmillotta.it/albania/calcio/ | title=Official RSSSF Site Covering the History of Albanian Football from 1913 until the Present }}</ref> Albania played its first international match against [[Yugoslavia national football team|Yugoslavia]] in 1946, which ended in a 3–2 home defeat at [[Qemal Stafa Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://eu-football.info/_match.php?id=6383|title=International football match Albania vs Yugoslavia report|publisher=EU-Football.info|access-date=15 October 2019|archive-date=17 November 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211117112921/https://eu-football.info/_match.php?id=6383|url-status=live}}</ref> In 1946, Albania also participated for the first time in the [[1946 Balkan Cup|Balkan Cup]] in which Albania won by beating [[Romania national football team|Romania]] 1–0 in the final.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://sot.com.al/dossier/ballkaniada-1946-kur-shqip%C3%ABria-u-shpall-kampione-e-ballkanit|title=Ballkaniada 1946, kur Shqipëria u shpall kampione e Ballkanit|trans-title=Balkan Cup 1946, when Albania was named champion of Balkan|publisher=Sot.com.al|language=en|date=9 October 2016|access-date=15 October 2019|archive-date=14 February 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190214075003/https://sot.com.al/dossier/ballkaniada-1946-kur-shqip%C3%ABria-u-shpall-kampione-e-ballkanit|url-status=live}}</ref> In 1954, Albania was one of the founding members of [[UEFA]]. Albania waited until 1962 to compete for the first time in a UEFA Euro Cup competition, and the reason being Albania got past the first leg against Greece, for political reasons forfeited the game winning twice 0-3 and 3-0 and in Total 6–0 on Aggregate, to qualify so for the first and only time so far, for the Round of 16 in a UEFA competition.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.ekskluzive.al/euro-1964-greqia-refuzoi-nga-ligji-i-luftes-u-kualifikua-shqiperia/|title="Euro 1964" / Greqia refuzoi nga "ligji i luftës", u kualifikua Shqipëria|trans-title="Euro 1964" / Greece refused due to "war law", Albania qualified|publisher=Ekskluzive.al|language=sq|date=30 October 2018|access-date=15 October 2019|archive-date=15 October 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191015010148/http://www.ekskluzive.al/euro-1964-greqia-refuzoi-nga-ligji-i-luftes-u-kualifikua-shqiperia/|url-status=live}}</ref><ref name="Dizdari2007">{{cite news|author=Besnik Dizdari|title=Ballkaniada 1946: Shqipëria – kampione e Ballkanit ne startin e "Luftës së ftohtë" në Evropë|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=DadPygAACAAJ|year=2007|publisher=Rozafat|isbn=978-99943-979-0-7|pages=2–10|language=sq|access-date=5 April 2016}}</ref><ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.uefa.com/uefaeuro/history/teams/2--albania/ | title=The official website for European football }}</ref> At the end of the tournament Albania ranked 9th in Europe.<ref name=Armillotta@Panorama>{{cite web|url=http://www.panorama.com.al/sport/shqiperia-ne-fazen-finale-nuk-eshte-hera-e-pare/|title=Shqipëria në fazën finale? Nuk është hera e parë!|trans-title=Albania in finale phase, its not the first time|publisher=Panorama Sport|language=sq|date=14 October 2015|access-date=15 October 2019|archive-date=15 October 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191015010306/http://www.panorama.com.al/sport/shqiperia-ne-fazen-finale-nuk-eshte-hera-e-pare/|url-status=live}}</ref><ref name="UEFA website on Albania">{{cite web|last1=UEFA|title=Victories of Albania until Euro2016 qualifiers|url=https://www.uefa.com/uefaeuro/history/teams/2--albania/|access-date=17 October 2015|archive-date=17 October 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20151017114130/http://www.uefa.com/uefaeuro/season=2016/teams/team=2/profile/|url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|title=Dr. Armillotta ka të drejtë|url=http://www.panorama-sport.com/dr-armillotta-ka-te-drejte/|access-date=17 October 2015|publisher=Panorama-Sport|date=15 October 2015|language=sq|author=Besnik Dizdari|archive-date=18 October 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20151018172258/http://www.panorama-sport.com/dr-armillotta-ka-te-drejte/|url-status=live}}</ref> Albania participated at the [[1964 Summer Olympics]] in the [[Football at the 1964 Summer Olympics – Men's qualification|Men's qualification]] tournament, and closely lost both matches against [[Bulgaria national football team|Bulgaria]] in the preliminary round.<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.rsssf.org/tableso/ol1964q.html | title=Games of the XVIII. Olympiad – Football Qualifying Tournament | access-date=3 February 2023 | archive-date=3 March 2016 | archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160303205402/http://www.rsssf.com/tableso/ol1964q.html | url-status=live }}</ref> They would participate for the second and last time at the [[1972 Summer Olympics]] in which they faced [[Romania national football team|Romania]] at the [[Football at the 1972 Summer Olympics – Men's European Qualifiers|European qualifiers]] in the playoffs. Albania would lose both matches narrowly, 2–1 in the first leg and at home in the second leg.<ref>{{cite web | url=http://www.panorama.com.al/sport/besnik-dizdari-e-verteta-e-pjesemarrjes-se-shqiperise-ne-lojerat-olimpike/ | title=Besnik Dizdari: E vërteta e pjesëmarrjes së Shqipërisë në Lojërat Olimpike | access-date=7 February 2022 | archive-date=5 February 2022 | archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220205161343/http://www.panorama.com.al/sport/besnik-dizdari-e-verteta-e-pjesemarrjes-se-shqiperise-ne-lojerat-olimpike/ | url-status=live }}</ref><ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.rsssf.org/tableso/ol1972q.html | title=Games of the XX. Olympiad – Football Qualifying Tournament | access-date=3 February 2023 | archive-date=11 July 2022 | archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220711203917/https://www.rsssf.org/tableso/ol1972q.html | url-status=live }}</ref>
[[File:Pullë e Kampionatit Europian të Futbollit (1984).png|thumb|234x234px|Albanian stamp honouring [[UEFA Euro 1984|Euro 1984]].]]

Albania participated for the first time in a qualifying phase of a [[FIFA World Cup|World Cup]] in [[1966 FIFA World Cup|1966]]. The team was drawn in the [[1966 FIFA World Cup qualification (UEFA – Group 5)|Group 5]], finishing in the last position with only one point from six matches. In the [[UEFA Euro 1968 qualifying|qualifiers]] of [[UEFA Euro 1968]], Albania drew 0–0 against [[West Germany national football team|West Germany]], denying the Germans a chance to participate in the finals.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://opinion.al/euro-1968-kur-kombetarja-e-shqiperise-eliminonte-gjermanine/|title=Euro 1968/ Kur Kombëtarja e Shqipërisë eliminonte Gjermaninë|trans-title=Euro 1968/ When Albania national team eliminated Germany|publisher=Opinion.al|language=sq|date=13 May 2016|access-date=15 October 2019|archive-date=15 October 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191015011809/https://opinion.al/euro-1968-kur-kombetarja-e-shqiperise-eliminonte-gjermanine/|url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.panorama.com.al/sport/1967-risite-e-te-vertetave-te-shqiperia-gjermania-0-0/|title=1967: Risitë e të vërtetave të Shqipëria-Gjermania 0–0…|trans-title=1967: The innovations of the truths of Albania-Germany 0–0…|publisher=Panorama Sport|language=sq|date=19 December 2017|access-date=15 October 2019|archive-date=15 October 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191015011801/http://www.panorama.com.al/sport/1967-risite-e-te-vertetave-te-shqiperia-gjermania-0-0/|url-status=live}}</ref> Albania did not participate in [[1970 FIFA World Cup|World Cup 1970]], [[UEFA Euro 1976]], [[1978 FIFA World Cup|World Cup 1978]] and [[UEFA Euro 1980]] qualifying for unknown political reasons.

After six years without playing any international matches, Albania entered the [[1982 FIFA World Cup|1982 World Cup]], managing to finish second-to-last in the qualifying stages. For qualifying for the World Cup four years later, Albania beat [[Belgium national football team|Belgium]] in [[Tirana]] 2–0 as well as drawing [[Poland national football team|Poland]] in [[Mielec]] 2–2.

===21st century===
In the 2002 World Cup [[2002 FIFA World Cup qualification – UEFA Group 9|qualifiers]], Albania only beat [[Greece national football team|Greece]]. [[UEFA Euro 2004 qualifying Group 10|For the Euros two years later]], Albania beat [[Russia national football team|Russia]] 3–1 at [[Loro Boriçi Stadium]].<ref name="First match">{{cite news|url=https://www.uefa.com/uefaeuro/history/news/0254-0d7b3a20b7dd-0f66848ad46a-1000--flying-start-for-briegel/|title=Flying start for Briegel|publisher=[[UEFA]]|date=30 March 2003|access-date=5 April 2016|archive-date=8 March 2016|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160308213011/http://www.uefa.com/uefaeuro/season=2004/matches/round=1579/match=69051/postmatch/report/index.html|url-status=live}}</ref> This match was also the debut of the German coach [[Hans-Peter Briegel]] who led Albania to an undefeated run in home matches. Despite good results, Albania finished in the penultimate spot with only eight points. Albania defeated Greece 2–1 two months after they won [[Euro 2004]].<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.uefa.com/news/newsid=223027.html|title=Albania eclipse European champions|publisher=[[UEFA]]|date=5 September 2004|access-date=5 April 2016|archive-date=6 November 2016|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20161106150945/http://www.uefa.com/news/newsid=223027.html|url-status=dead}}</ref>

In [[UEFA Euro 2008 qualifying Group G|Euro 2008 qualifying]], Albania defeated [[Luxembourg national football team|Luxembourg]] twice, and also drew 2–2 with [[Belarus national football team|Belarus]] and 0–0 away with [[Bulgaria national football team|Bulgaria]]. Albania's Euro campaign ended with a 6–1 loss away to [[Romania national football team|Romania]] which resulted in the resignation of coach [[Otto Barić]] and his assistant.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://fshf.org/lajme_m.aspx?id=359|title=Baric: Po largohem|publisher=FSHF.org|language=sq|access-date=5 April 2016}}{{dead link|date=April 2016}}</ref> In December 2007, [[Arie Haan]] was named Albania's head coach by signing a two-year contract for the [[2010 FIFA World Cup qualification – UEFA Group 1|2010 World Cup qualifiers]]. Albania won only one match, with seven points from ten matches and Haan was replaced by [[Josip Kuže]] in May 2009 following the end of the campaign.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.tvkoha.tv/1300/josip-kuzhe-trajner-i-shqiperise.html |title=Televizioni Koha – JOSIP KUZHE TRAJNER I SHQIPËRISË – Sport |language=sq |publisher=Televizioni Koha |access-date=19 June 2015 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150619153814/http://www.tvkoha.tv/1300/josip-kuzhe-trajner-i-shqiperise.html |archive-date=19 June 2015 }}</ref> However, Kuže failed to lead Albania to better results as the team ended the [[UEFA Euro 2012 qualifying Group D|Euro 2012 qualifying]] with nine points from ten matches. With Kuže in charge, Albania recorded its second biggest win in history by defeating [[Cyprus national football team|Cyprus]] 6–1 at home.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.yllpress.com/4616/me-ne-fund-shperthen-shqiperia-fiton-6-me-1-kunder-qipros.html|title=Më në fund, shpërthen Shqipëria, fiton 6 me 1 kunder Qipros|publisher=Yllpress.com|language=sq|date=13 October 2009|access-date=25 March 2016|archive-date=25 October 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20201025022218/http://www.yllpress.com/4616/me-ne-fund-shperthen-shqiperia-fiton-6-me-1-kunder-qipros.html|url-status=live}}</ref> Josip Kuže parted ways with Albania after three and a half years, and in December 2011, Italian coach [[Gianni de Biasi]] replaced him.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.panorama.com.al/kush-eshte-trajneri-i-ri-i-kombetares-shqiptare/|title=Kush është trajneri i ri i Kombëtares shqiptare|publisher=Panorama.al|access-date=6 April 2016|date=14 December 2011|language=sq|archive-date=3 October 2016|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20161003092352/http://www.panorama.com.al/kush-eshte-trajneri-i-ri-i-kombetares-shqiptare/|url-status=live}}</ref> Albania was, at one point, second in the [[2014 FIFA World Cup qualification|2014 World Cup qualifying]] group, but then lost to [[Slovenia national football team|Slovenia]] and [[Iceland national football team|Iceland]], as well as at home against [[Switzerland national football team|Switzerland]], and drawing in Cyprus.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.panorama-sport.com/revolucioni-i-kombetares-atdhetaria-dhe-pseudoatdhetaria/|title="Revolucioni" i Kombëtares, atdhetaria dhe "pseudoatdhetaria"|publisher=Panorama Sport|language=sq|author=Besnik Dizdari|access-date=4 April 2016|date=4 April 2015}}</ref> [[File:AUT vs. ALB 2016-03-26 (004).jpg|thumb|Albania squad wearing the visitors' uniform, 2016.|281x281px]]Albania began [[UEFA Euro 2016 qualifying Group I]] with an emphatic 1–0 away win against [[Portugal national football team|Portugal]],<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.panorama-sport.com/239815/|title="Bekim" kuqezi në Aveiro, Shqipëria shokon Portugalinë|date=7 September 2014|publisher=[[Panorama Sport]]|language=sq|access-date=7 September 2014|archive-date=13 April 2016|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160413155256/http://www.panorama-sport.com/239815/|url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |last1=Amofa |first1=Richard |date=19 June 2016 |title=Euro 2016: Albania 0–1 Romania – Armando Sadiku scores the only goal to seal his country's first ever win at a major competition |url=http://www.telegraph.co.uk/football/2016/06/19/albania-vs-romania-euro-2016---live/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160619152011/http://www.telegraph.co.uk/football/2016/06/19/albania-vs-romania-euro-2016---live/ |archive-date=19 June 2016 |access-date=19 June 2016 |work=telegraph.co.uk}}</ref> followed by a 1–1 draw against [[Denmark national football team|Denmark]] at the newly renovated [[Elbasan Arena]].<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.albdreams.net/flirtuam-me-fitoren-por-bashkekryesojme-me-danimarken/|title="Flirtuam" me fitoren, por bashkëkryesojmë me Danimarkën|publisher=Albdreams.net|language=sq|date=11 October 2014|access-date=11 October 2014|archive-date=13 April 2016|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160413154219/http://www.albdreams.net/flirtuam-me-fitoren-por-bashkekryesojme-me-danimarken/|url-status=dead}}</ref> They then beat Serbia 3–0 in [[Serbia v Albania (UEFA Euro 2016 qualifying)|a match full of incidents]]. They finally qualified for a [[UEFA Euro 2016|UEFA European Championship]] after beating [[Armenia national football team|Armenia]] in Yerevan 0–3, its first appearance in the country's history at a major men's football tournament.<ref>{{cite news |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/football/34501117 |title=Armenia 0–3 Albania – BBC Sport |work=BBC Sport |access-date=29 May 2020}}</ref> 
{{UEFA Euro 2016 qualifying group tables|Group I|show_matches=no}}

In the tournament itself, Albania lost 1–0 to [[Switzerland national football team|Switzerland]] to an early header in the 5th Minute from [[Fabian Schär]] despite being one man down to a Red Card for [[Lorik Cana]] Albania held its ground as they came close to an equalizer as [[Shkëlzen Gashi]] as he missed an sitter in front of goalie [[Yann Sommer]].<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/football/36450647 | title=Albania 0-1 Switzerland | work=BBC Sport | date=10 June 2016 }}</ref><ref>{{cite web | url=https://boldnews.al/2016/06/11/euro-2016shqiperia-humbet-0-1-perballe-zvicres/ | title=Euro 2016/Shqipëria humbet 0-1 përballë Zvicrës | date=11 June 2016 }}</ref> In the following match they lost 2–0 to the hosts [[France national football team|France]] despite an close fought match being able to defend against the favourites until the last minutes conceding two goals from [[Dimitri Payet]] and [[Antoine Griezmann|Griezmann]].<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/football/36475991 | title=France 2-0 Albania | work=BBC Sport | date=14 June 2016 }}</ref><ref>{{cite web | url=https://euronews.al/shqiperia-humb-0-2-kunder-frances-inagurohet-stadiumi-i-ri/ | title=Shqipëria humb 0-2 kundër Francës, inagurohet stadiumi i ri | date=17 November 2019 }}</ref> While they beat in the third Group match Romania 1–0 by a header scored from [[Armando Sadiku]] before halftime, securing Albania's first ever win in a [[UEFA European Championship]].<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/football/36509864 | title=Romania 0-1 Albania | work=BBC Sport | date=18 June 2016 }}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.respublica.al/2016/06/19/shqip%C3%ABria-fiton-ndaj-rumanis%C3%AB-goli-i-par%C3%AB-historik-n%C3%AB-europian-nga-armando-sadiku|title=Shqipëria fiton ndaj Rumanisë. Goli i parë historik në Europian, nga Armando Sadiku &#124; ResPublica|website=www.respublica.al}}</ref> The team finished last among the third-placed teams and were eliminated in the group stage.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.fourfourtwo.com/news/albania-out-euro-2016-after-portugal-draw-hungary|title=Albania out of Euro 2016 after Portugal draw with Hungary|publisher=Fourfourtwo.com|date=22 June 2016|access-date=9 October 2016|archive-date=9 October 2016|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20161009193113/http://www.fourfourtwo.com/news/albania-out-euro-2016-after-portugal-draw-hungary|url-status=live}}</ref>

In [[2018 FIFA World Cup qualification (UEFA)|2018 World Cup qualification]], Albania was drawn in a [[2018 FIFA World Cup qualification – UEFA Group G|group]] with [[Spain national football team|Spain]], [[Italy national football team|Italy]], [[Liechtenstein national football team|Liechtenstein]], [[Macedonia national football team|Macedonia]], and [[Israel national football team|Israel]]. Despite the difficult draw, they placed third in the group, their best result in history without qualifying for the final tournament. During this time, manager [[Gianni De Biasi]] resigned after disputes with the FSHF and was replaced by [[Christian Panucci]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://en.as.com/en/2017/07/19/football/1500470638_776017.html|title=Panucci named Albania coach|publisher=AS|date=17 July 2017|access-date=15 July 2020|archive-date=17 April 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210417001106/https://en.as.com/en/2017/07/19/football/1500470638_776017.html|url-status=dead}}</ref> Albania played poorly in the [[2018–19 UEFA Nations League]], winning only once against Israel and losing the return leg as well as both matches against [[Scotland national football team|Scotland]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/football/46113905|title=Albania 0–4 Scotland|publisher=BBC Sport|author=Andrew Southwick|date=17 November 2018|access-date=15 July 2020|archive-date=17 July 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200717214119/https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/football/46113905|url-status=live}}</ref> Panucci would be sacked after a 2–0 defeat to [[Turkey national football team|Turkey]] in the opening match of [[UEFA Euro 2020 qualifying]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.tribalfootball.com/articles/sacked-christian-panucci-dumped-by-albania-4273957|title=SACKED! Christian Panucci dumped by Albania|publisher=Tribal Football|author=Carlos Volcano|access-date=15 July 2020|archive-date=29 October 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20201029020720/https://www.tribalfootball.com/articles/sacked-christian-panucci-dumped-by-albania-4273957|url-status=live}}</ref> [[Edoardo Reja]] replaced Panucci as Albania faced home and away series against both [[2018 FIFA World Cup]] champions [[France national football team|France]] and [[Iceland national football team|Iceland]] as well as the return leg against [[Turkey national football team|Turkey]].<ref>{{cite news |title=Albania hires Edoardo Reja to coach its national team |url=https://www.usatoday.com/story/sports/soccer/2019/04/17/albania-hires-italian-edoardo-reja-for-its-national-team/39355433/ |work=USA TODAY |access-date=4 July 2019 |archive-date=4 July 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190704180135/https://www.usatoday.com/story/sports/soccer/2019/04/17/albania-hires-italian-edoardo-reja-for-its-national-team/39355433/ |url-status=live }}</ref> In the end, Albania placed fourth in the group, failing to qualify.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.ipn.md/en/moldovas-national-team-was-also-outclassed-by-albania-7979_1068842.html|title=Moldova's national team was also outclassed by Albania|website=ipn.md|date=15 October 2019|access-date=15 July 2020|archive-date=28 January 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210128154306/https://www.ipn.md/en/moldovas-national-team-was-also-outclassed-by-albania-7979_1068842.html|url-status=live}}</ref>

After narrowly missing out on qualification for the [[2022 FIFA World Cup]] in Qatar, finishing third in [[2022 FIFA World Cup qualification – UEFA Group I|Group I]],<ref>{{Cite web |date=2021-11-15 |title=Shqipëria nuk kualifikohet në Katar, por arrinë rezultatin historik në kualifikime |trans-title=Albania does not qualify for Qatar, but achieves a historic result in the qualifiers |url=https://albanianpost.com/shqiperia-nuk-kualifikohet-ne-katar-por-arrine-rezultatin-historik-ne-kualifikime/ |access-date=28 June 2024 |website=Albanian Post |language=sq}}</ref> Albania were drawn in [[UEFA Euro 2024 qualifying Group E]] alongside the [[Czech Republic national football team|Czech Republic]], [[Poland national football team|Poland]], [[Faroe Islands national football team|Faroe Islands]] and [[Moldova national football team|Moldova]].<ref>{{cite web |last=Krasniqi |first=Altin |url=https://www.dukagjini.com/shqiperia-ne-nje-grup-te-lehte-per-euro-2024/ |title=Shqipëria në një grup të "lehtë" për Euro 2024 |trans-title=Albania in a "favourable" group for Euro 2024 |website=Dukagjini |date=9 October 2022 |access-date=28 June 2024 |language=sq}}</ref> Albania went unbeaten in the qualifying campaign and secured qualification for the [[UEFA Euro 2024]], marking their second appearance at a [[UEFA European Championship]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=2024-06-06 |title=Who has qualified for UEFA EURO 2024? |url=https://www.uefa.com/euro2024/news/0286-193531eba9a8-763890f6436d-1000--who-has-qualified-for-uefa-euro-2024/ |access-date=28 June 2024 |website=UEFA |language=en}}</ref>

At [[UEFA Euro 2024]],<ref>{{Cite web |last=Semini |first=Llazar |date=2023-11-22 |title=South American coaches lead Albania to Euro 2024 spot. Sylvinho gets a medal from prime minister |url=https://apnews.com/article/brazilian-argentinian-coach-albania-euro-2024-632483c8669fa984aa7cfca59de2b2ef |access-date=11 February 2024 |website=AP News |language=en}}</ref> Albania were drawn into a "[[group of death]]" alongside [[Spain national football team|Spain]], [[Italy national football team|Italy]] and [[Croatia national football team|Croatia]], all of whom are European and World Cup medalists. After losses to Spain and Italy and a 2–2 draw against Croatia, Albania finished bottom of the group and were eliminated in the group stage.<ref>{{Cite news |last1=Anka |first1=Carl |last2=Corrigan |first2=Dermot |title=Croatia 2 Albania 2 - How did Croatia let it slip? How far can Albania go? Unluckiest own goal? |url=https://www.nytimes.com/athletic/5572674/2024/06/19/croatia-albania-euro-2024/ |access-date=27 June 2024 |work=The New York Times |language=en-US |issn=0362-4331}}</ref>

Albania competed in the [[2024–25 UEFA Nations League B|2024–25 UEFA Nations League B]], where they finished bottom of their group and were relegated to League C.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.uefa.com/uefanationsleague/standings/ |title=UEFA Nations League standings |publisher=UEFA |access-date=3 April 2026}}</ref> They subsequently participated in the [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification (UEFA)|2026 FIFA World Cup qualification]], finishing second in [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – UEFA Group K|Group K]] with four wins without conceding, two draws, and two defeats against [[England national football team|England]], thereby securing qualification to the [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – UEFA second round|play-off]] stage for the first time in their history.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.fifa.com/en/tournaments/mens/worldcup/canadamexicousa2026/qualifiers/uefa/standings |title=European Qualifiers standings |publisher=FIFA |access-date=3 April 2026}}</ref> In the play-off, Albania was eliminated following a 2–1 defeat to [[Poland national football team|Poland]], despite initially taking the lead.<ref>{{cite news |url=https://www.panorama.com.al/sport/video-endrra-kuqezi-behet-makth-ne-10-minuta-gabimet-ne-te-dy-portat-eliminojne-shqiperine-me-permbysje-ne-polonine/ |title=VIDEO/ Ëndrra kuqezi bëhet makth në 10 minuta, gabimet në të dy portat eliminojnë Shqipërinë me përmbysje në Poloninë |trans-title=VIDEO/ Red-and-Black dream turns into a nightmare in 10 minutes, mistakes at both ends eliminate Albania in Poland |work=Panorama Sport |date=26 March 2026 |access-date=3 April 2026}}</ref>

==Team image==
===Nicknames===
The team's nickname is commonly known as "''Kuqezinjtë''" ("The Red and Blacks") During the period before 2016, the national teams of Albania and Kosovo exchanged players with each other due to the complexities of descent brought on by Kosovo's independence, leading supporters to refer to each team as the other team's "reserve" side.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.panorama.com.al/sport/kosova-a-kosova-b-2-2-ose-kosova-shqiperia-30-6/|title=Kosova A – "Kosova B" 2–2 ose Kosova – Shqipëria 30–6!|trans-title=Kosovo A – "Kosovo B" 2–2 or Kosovo – Albania 30–6!|language=Albanian|author=Besnik Dizdari|publisher=[[Panorama Sport]]|date=18 November 2015|access-date=13 July 2021|archive-date=2 November 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211102165944/http://www.panorama.com.al/sport/kosova-a-kosova-b-2-2-ose-kosova-shqiperia-30-6/|url-status=live}}</ref>

===Kits===
{{Commons|Albania national football team kits}}
Albania's uniform colors are red and black, mirroring the [[Flag of Albania|nation's flag]]. The team typically wears red shirts, black shorts and red socks. Away kits are usually all-white, with red and black trimming.

Over the years, the national team has sported kit wear from renowned sportswear brands such as [[Legea]], [[Puma (brand)|Puma]], [[Nike, Inc.|Nike]] and [[Adidas]], to name a few. The partnership with Puma began in 1996 and lasted nine years. Nike outfitted the team during the [[UEFA Euro 2008 qualifying|UEFA Euro 2008]] and the [[2010 FIFA World Cup qualification|2010 World Cup qualifiers]]. A collaboration with Adidas followed, in a contract signing which allowed it to manufacture kits for the [[UEFA Euro 2012 qualifying|Euro 2012 qualifiers]], as well as the [[2014 FIFA World Cup qualification|2014 World Cup]] and the [[UEFA Euro 2016 qualifying|Euro 2016 qualifiers]].{{citation needed|date=June 2024}}

On 24 March 2016, the [[Albanian Football Federation]] unveiled a New Jersey for the national team players, set to be worn during the forthcoming [[UEFA Euro 2016]] tournament, hosted that year in France. Manufactured by [[Macron (sportswear)|Macron]], the jersey redesign, labelled "Triumfi," was conceptualised by Tirana-based studio PIK Creative.<ref name=FirstSix>{{cite news|url=https://www.syri.net/2016/01/29/historia-e-fanellave-te-kombetares-nga-puma-tek-adidasfoto/|title=Historia e fanellave të kombëtares, nga Puma tek Adidas/FOTO|trans-title=The history of the national team jerseys, from Puma to Adidas/PHOTO|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160203045527/https://www.syri.net/2016/01/29/historia-e-fanellave-te-kombetares-nga-puma-tek-adidasfoto/|language=Albanian|website=Syri.net|date=29 January 2016|archive-date=3 February 2016}}</ref><ref name=SecondSix>{{cite web|url=https://fshf.org/sq/macron-sponsor-teknik-i-kombetares-edhe-per-6-vite-duka-nje-nga-partneret-tane-me-te-rendesishem/|title="Macron", sponsor teknik i Kombëtares edhe për 6 vite, Duka: Një nga partnerët tanë më të rëndësishëm|trans-title="Macron", technical sponsor of the national team for another 6 years, Duka: One of our most important partners|language=Albanian|publisher=[[Albanian Football Federation]]|date=9 June 2022|access-date=21 August 2022|archive-date=21 August 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220821164258/https://fshf.org/sq/macron-sponsor-teknik-i-kombetares-edhe-per-6-vite-duka-nje-nga-partneret-tane-me-te-rendesishem/|url-status=live}}</ref>

{{Lineage
| 1|-1|{{resize|110%|'''Kit wear suppliers'''}}|
| 2| 1|{{flagicon|GER}}<br>[[Adidas]]<br>''(1988)''|
| 3| 1|{{flagicon|ENG}}<br>[[Umbro]]<br>''(1992–93)''|
| 4| 1|{{flagicon|GER}}<br>[[Uhlsport]]<br>''(1994–95)''|
| 5| 1|{{flagicon|GER}}<br>[[Puma (brand)|Puma]]<br>''(1996–04)''|
| 6| 1|{{flagicon|ENG}}<br>[[Umbro]]<br>''(2004–07)''|
| 7| 1|{{flagicon|USA}}<br>[[Nike, Inc.|Nike]]<br>''(2008–10)''|
| 8| 1|{{flagicon|ITA}}<br>[[Legea]]<br>''(2010–12)''|
| 9| 1|{{flagicon|GER}}<br>[[Adidas]]<br>''(2012–16)''|
|10| 1|{{flagicon|ITA}}<br>[[Macron (sportswear)|Macron]]<br>''(2016–27)''|
}}

===Home stadium===
{{Main|Arena Kombëtare|Elbasan Arena|Loro Boriçi Stadium}}

{{See also|Qemal Stafa Stadium}}
[[File:Air Albania Stadium 2024.jpg|thumb|[[Arena Kombëtare]] is the home ground of the national team.]]
For most of Albania's history, the home stadium was [[Qemal Stafa Stadium]] in [[Tirana]]. Construction started in April 1939 during the [[Italian protectorate of Albania (1939–1943)|Italy regime]], lasted for three years but stopped briefly in August 1943 following the [[Fall of the Fascist regime in Italy|fall of fascist regime]].<ref name="History">{{cite web|url=http://www.panorama.com.al/opinion-dizdari-ne-panorama-qemal-stafa-historia-70-vjecare-e-stadiumit-kombetar/|title="Qemal Stafa", historia 70-vjeçare e Stadiumit Kombëtar (Pjesa 1)|trans-title="Qemal Stafa", the 70-year history of the national stadium (Part 1)|publisher=Gazeta Panorama|language=sq|date=10 May 2016|access-date=18 October 2019|archive-date=18 October 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191018203838/http://www.panorama.com.al/opinion-dizdari-ne-panorama-qemal-stafa-historia-70-vjecare-e-stadiumit-kombetar/|url-status=live}}</ref> The stadium had an [[Olympic Stadium]] shape, as idealised by [[Gherardo Bosio]], a young fascist architect from [[Florence]].<ref name="Qemal Stafa Stadium">{{cite web|url=http://www.standard.al/2014/09/26/historia-e-ndertimit-te-stadiumit-qemal-stafa/|title=Historia e ndërtimit të stadiumit "Qemal Stafa"|work=Gazeta Standard Online |trans-title=History of Stadium "Qemal Stafa" construction|publisher=Standard.al|language=sq|date=26 September 2014|access-date=18 October 2019|archive-date=18 October 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191018203819/http://www.standard.al/2014/09/26/historia-e-ndertimit-te-stadiumit-qemal-stafa/|url-status=live}}</ref> The stadium's initial capacity was 15,000, due to the fact that Tirana at that time had only 60,000 inhabitants.<ref name="Qemal Stafa Stadium"/> It was named after [[Qemal Stafa]], a [[Hero of Albania]] in [[World War II]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://gazetamapo.al/kush-ishte-qemal-stafa-99-vjetori-i-lindjes/|title=Kush ishte Qemal Stafa? 99-vjetori i lindjes|trans-title=Who was Qemal Stafa? 99th anniversary of birth|publisher=Gazeta Mapo|language=sq|date=20 March 2019|access-date=18 October 2019|archive-date=24 September 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190924090355/https://gazetamapo.al/kush-ishte-qemal-stafa-99-vjetori-i-lindjes/|url-status=live}}</ref> The stadium was inaugurated officially on 7 October 1946 when Albania played its first competitive match against Yugoslavia.<ref name="Stadium1">{{cite web|url=https://eu-football.info/_venue.php?id=803|title=Stadiumi Kombëtar Qemal Stafa, Tiranë|website=eu-football.info|access-date=23 October 2017|archive-date=24 October 2017|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20171024042721/https://eu-football.info/_venue.php?id=803|url-status=live}}</ref> Ever since then, a further 130 international matches took place in the stadium, with the last being a friendly against Georgia in November 2015.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.panorama.com.al/sport/kombetarja-luan-ndeshjen-e-fundit-ne-qemal-stafa/|title=Kombëtarja luan ndeshjen e fundit në "Qemal Stafa"|trans-title=The national team plays the last match in "Qemal Stafa"|publisher=Panorama Sport|language=sq|date=16 November 2015|access-date=18 October 2019|archive-date=18 October 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191018203949/http://www.panorama.com.al/sport/kombetarja-luan-ndeshjen-e-fundit-ne-qemal-stafa/|url-status=live}}</ref> In 2005, Cecilia de Marco and Elisabetta Lorusso, two young Italian students, called the stadium as "one of the strongest symbols of Italian impact in Albania".<ref name="History"/> In November 2013, Qemal Stafa Stadium was shut down by FIFA for not fulfilling international standards.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://sport.albeu.com/kombetarja/uefa-djeg-edhe-ruzhdi-bizhuten-e-niko-dovanen/132437/|title=UEFA "djeg" edhe "Ruzhdi Bizhutën" e "Niko Dovanën"|publisher=Sport.albeu.com|language=sq|date=21 November 2013|access-date=19 June 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140526000601/http://sport.albeu.com/kombetarja/uefa-djeg-edhe-ruzhdi-bizhuten-e-niko-dovanen/132437/|archive-date=26 May 2014|url-status=dead}}</ref> The stadium's demolition started in June 2016, and it was announced that it would be replaced by [[Arena Kombëtare]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://tvklan.al/nis-prishja-e-stadiumit-qemal-stafa/|title=Nis prishja e stadiumit "Qemal Stafa"|trans-title=The demolition of "Qemal Stafa" Stadium starts|publisher=TV Klan|language=sq|date=9 June 2016|access-date=18 October 2019|archive-date=18 October 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191018203822/https://tvklan.al/nis-prishja-e-stadiumit-qemal-stafa/|url-status=live}}</ref>

Albania has played home matches at other venues; outside Tirana, the national team has played matches in [[Flamurtari Stadium]] in [[Vlorë]],<ref name="Stadium6">{{cite web|url=https://eu-football.info/_match.php?id=8772|title=Stadiumi Flamurtari, Vlorë|website=eu-football.info|access-date=14 January 2018|archive-date=14 January 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180114184907/https://eu-football.info/_match.php?id=8772|url-status=live}}</ref> [[Tomori Stadium]] in [[Berat]],<ref name="Stadium5">{{cite news |title=Stadiumi Tomori, Berat |website=eu-football.info |url=https://eu-football.info/_venue.php?id=994 |url-status=live |access-date=23 October 2017 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20171024043414/https://eu-football.info/_venue.php?id=994 |archive-date=24 October 2017}}</ref> [[Niko Dovana Stadium]] in [[Durrës]],<ref name="Stadium4">{{cite web|url=https://eu-football.info/_venue.php?id=1841|title=Stadiumi Niko Dovana, Durrës|website=eu-football.info|access-date=23 October 2017|archive-date=24 October 2017|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20171024043121/https://eu-football.info/_venue.php?id=1841|url-status=live}}</ref> [[Loro Boriçi Stadium]] in [[Shkodër]]<ref name="Stadium3">{{cite web|url=https://eu-football.info/_venue.php?id=541|title=Loro Boriçi Stadium, Shkodër|website=eu-football.info|access-date=23 October 2017|archive-date=3 August 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200803091754/https://eu-football.info/_venue.php?id=541|url-status=live}}</ref> and [[Elbasan Arena]] in [[Elbasan]].<ref name="Stadium2">{{cite web|url=https://eu-football.info/_venue.php?id=2350|title=Elbasan Arena, Elbasan|website=eu-football.info|access-date=23 October 2017|archive-date=24 October 2017|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20171024043550/https://eu-football.info/_venue.php?id=2350|url-status=live}}</ref> In February 2014, renovations for Elbasan Arena (at the time Ruzhdi Bizhuta Stadium) started.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://sport.albeu.com/kryetitull/nis-rindertimi-i-stadiumit-ruzhdi-bizhuta-foto-lajm/141519/|title=Nis rindërtimi i stadiumit "Ruzhdi Bizhuta" (FOTO LAJM)|trans-title=The reconstruction of "Ruzhdi Buzhuta" stadium starts (Photo News)|publisher=Sport.albeu.com|language=sq|date=17 February 2015|access-date=18 October 2019|archive-date=18 October 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191018203827/http://sport.albeu.com/kryetitull/nis-rindertimi-i-stadiumit-ruzhdi-bizhuta-foto-lajm/141519/|url-status=live}}</ref> The work lasted for seven months, and the stadium was inaugurated on 9 October when Albania played a friendly match against [[KF Elbasani]]'s under-19 squad; Albania won the match 17–0.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.panorama.com.al/sport/goleade-e-kombetares-ne-inaugurimin-e-stadiumit/|title=Goleadë e Kombëtares në inaugurimin e stadiumit|trans-title=Goal-fest of national team in the stadium's inauguration|publisher=Panorama Sport|language=sq|date=9 October 2014|access-date=18 October 2019|archive-date=18 October 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191018203845/http://www.panorama.com.al/sport/goleade-e-kombetares-ne-inaugurimin-e-stadiumit/|url-status=live}}</ref> The official inauguration occurred two days later in the [[UEFA Euro 2016 qualifying|Euro 2016 qualifying]] match against [[Denmark national football team|Denmark]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.panorama.com.al/shqiperi-danimarke-ndeshja-e-pare-ne-elbasan-arena-ja-cmimi-i-biletave/|title=Shqipëri- Danimarkë, ndeshja e parë në "Elbasan Arena". Ja çmimi i biletave|trans-title=Albania-Denmark, the first match in "Elbasan Arena". Here are the ticket's prizes|publisher=Gazeta Panorama|language=sq|date=22 September 2014|access-date=18 October 2019|archive-date=13 October 2016|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20161013031910/http://www.panorama.com.al/shqiperi-danimarke-ndeshja-e-pare-ne-elbasan-arena-ja-cmimi-i-biletave/|url-status=live}}</ref>

The first international match at Loro Boriçi Stadium was played on 29 March 2003 against [[Russia national football team|Russia]], with Albania winning 3–1.<ref name="First match"/> In October 2014, [[Prime Minister of Albania]], [[Edi Rama]], promised the reconstruction of the stadium.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.cna.al/2015/04/01/rama-se-shpejti-nis-ndertimi-i-stadiumit-loro-borici/|title=Rama: Së shpejti nis ndërtimi i stadiumit 'Loro Borici'|trans-title=Rama: Construction of ‘Loro Borici’ Stadium Coming Soon|publisher=City News Albania|language=sq|date=1 April 2015|access-date=18 October 2019|archive-date=18 October 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191018203843/https://www.cna.al/2015/04/01/rama-se-shpejti-nis-ndertimi-i-stadiumit-loro-borici/|url-status=live}}</ref> The stadium's construction started in May 2015 and finished in August 2016.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://tvklan.al/stadiumi-loro-borici-eshte-gati-per-kombetaren-si-ti-gjeni-biletat/|title=Stadiumi "Loro Boriçi" është gati për Kombëtaren, si t'i gjeni biletat|trans-title="Loro Boriçi" stadium is ready for the national team, how to find the tickets|publisher=TV Klan|language=sq|date=30 August 2016|access-date=18 October 2019|archive-date=18 October 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191018203819/https://tvklan.al/stadiumi-loro-borici-eshte-gati-per-kombetaren-si-ti-gjeni-biletat/|url-status=live}}</ref> In 2016–17, the stadium served temporary as the home of [[Kosovo national football team|Kosovo national team]], due to stadiums in [[Mitrovica, Kosovo|Mitrovica]] and [[Pristina]] being renovated and not meeting UEFA standards.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://telesport.al/e-konfirmon-kryeministri-kosova-te-luaje-ne-loro-borici|title=E konfirmon kryeministri: Kosova do të luajë në "Loro Boriçi"|trans-title=The Prime Minister confirms: Kosovo will play in "Loro Boriçi"|publisher=Telesport.al|language=sq|author=Alfred Lleshi|date=11 August 2016|access-date=18 October 2019|archive-date=18 October 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191018205327/https://telesport.al/e-konfirmon-kryeministri-kosova-te-luaje-ne-loro-borici|url-status=live}}</ref>

===Rivalries===
====Kosovo====
{{main|Brotherly derby}}

This derby is otherwise known as Brotherly derby ({{langx|sq|Derbi vëllazëror}}), or the Brotherly ({{langx|sq|Vëllazërorja}}). The documented beginnings of this derby date back to World War II, specifically on 29 November 1942, where they played a [[Exhibition game#Association football|friendly match]] as part of the celebrations for the 30th anniversary of the independence of Albania. The match ended with a 2–0 win for Tirana.{{efn|The alternative name of the Albania national team that was used during this match.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://gazetasi.al/kalvari-sportiv-i-kosoves-dhe-miqesorja-e-harruar-me-jugosllavine/|title=Kosova, kalvari sportiv e miqësorja e harruar me Jugosllavinë|trans-title=Kosovo, the sports ordeal and the forgotten friendly with Yugoslavia|language=sq|author=Mendrit Shehu|website=Gazetasi|date=18 November 2019|access-date=23 June 2020|archive-date=3 August 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200803005151/https://gazetasi.al/kalvari-sportiv-i-kosoves-dhe-miqesorja-e-harruar-me-jugosllavine/|url-status=live}}</ref>}}<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.zemrashqiptare.net/news/1668/rp-0/act-print/rf-1/printo.html|title=Besnik Dizdari: Kur në Tiranë lindte Përfaqësuesja e Kosovës...(II)|trans-title=Besnik Dizdari: When in Tirana the Kosovo national team was born...(II)|language=sq|author=Besnik Dizdari|website=Zemra Shqiptare|date=12 December 2007|access-date=13 July 2021|archive-date=9 January 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220109044136/http://www.zemrashqiptare.net/news/1668/rp-0/act-print/rf-1/printo.html|url-status=live}}</ref><ref name="Besnik Dizdari Panorama Sport"/>

====Serbia====
{{main|Albania–Serbia football rivalry}}

This football rivalry between Albania and Serbia is mainly due to [[Albania–Serbia relations|historical tensions]] between the two countries. It has been described as "one of the fiercest rivalries in the world". During the [[Serbia v Albania (UEFA Euro 2016 qualifying)|Euro 2016 qualifying phase]], with Portugal, Denmark and Armenia, the Serbian crowd were chanting "kill, kill the Albanians"<ref>{{Cite web|last=Ibrulj|first=Sasa|date=15 October 2014|title=Serbia vs Albania: The inside story of the violence|url=http://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/football/news/serbia-vs-albania-inside-story-4441062|access-date=2021-06-30|website=mirror|language=en|archive-date=11 May 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210511120915/https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/football/news/serbia-vs-albania-inside-story-4441062|url-status=live}}</ref> and were throwing flares at the Albanian players, after which there was a clash between the two teams.<ref>{{Cite web|date=17 November 2014|title=The world's greatest international football rivalries – ranked and reviewed |author1=Nick Miller |author2=Nick Ames|url=http://www.theguardian.com/football/blog/2014/nov/17/football-international-rivalries-ranked-scotland-england|access-date=2021-06-30|website=The Guardian|language=en|archive-date=20 August 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210820024940/https://www.theguardian.com/football/blog/2014/nov/17/football-international-rivalries-ranked-scotland-england|url-status=live}}</ref>

Both countries were drawn in the same group for the [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – UEFA Group K|2026 FIFA World Cup qualification]], with England, Latvia and Andorra.

===Supporters===
{{Main|Tifozat Kuq e Zi}}

[[File:Tifozat Kuq e Zi ne Elbasan Arena.jpg|thumb|left|255px|A choreography of Tifozat Kuq e Zi in the national team's first match at [[Elbasan Arena]] against Denmark in October 2014]]
'''Tifozat Kuq e Zi''' (Red and Black Fans) is a non-profit [[association football|football]] supporters' association for the Albania national football team and various national team sportive activities.<ref name="TKZ">{{cite web|url=http://www.panorama.com.al/sport/tifozat-kuq-e-zi-ja-si-lindi-grupimi-i-famshem-i-kombetares/|title="Tifozat Kuq e Zi", ja si lindi grupimi i famshëm i Kombëtares|trans-title="Red and Black Fans", here's how the famous national team group born|publisher=Panorama Sport|language=sq|date=27 October 2015|access-date=19 October 2019|archive-date=19 October 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191019001912/http://www.panorama.com.al/sport/tifozat-kuq-e-zi-ja-si-lindi-grupimi-i-famshem-i-kombetares/|url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.panorama.com.al/sport/16-vjet-krenari-dhe-kontribut-per-kombetaren-tone-dite-historike-per-tifozat-kuq-e-zi/|title=16 vjet krenari dhe kontribut për kombëtaren tonë, ditë historike për "Tifozat Kuq e Zi"|trans-title=16 years pride and contribution for our national team, historical day for "Tifozat Kuq e Zi"|publisher=Panorama Sport|language=sq|date=23 December 2019|access-date=25 December 2019|archive-date=24 December 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191224231755/http://www.panorama.com.al/sport/16-vjet-krenari-dhe-kontribut-per-kombetaren-tone-dite-historike-per-tifozat-kuq-e-zi/|url-status=live}}</ref> It was founded on 25 December 2003.<ref name="TKZ"/> In cooperation with [[Albanian Football Federation|FSHF]], it organises trips for football fans to visit games, and develops and sells merchandise to support itself and fund sporting related projects.

Tifozat Kuq e Zi stands firm in the political view that [[Albanian people|Albanians]] should share only one national team and have continuous aspirations to join in one state (Një Komb, Një Kombëtare), i.e. unification of Albania, Kosovo, etc.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.gazeta-shqip.com/2014/01/21/tifozet-bojkot-ndaj-kosoves-nje-komb-nje-kombetare/|title=Tifozët, bojkot ndaj Kosovës: "Një komb, një kombëtare!"|trans-title=The fans, boycott towards Kosovo: "One nation, one national team!"|publisher=Gazeta Shqip|language=sq|author=Ermal Kuka|date=21 January 2014|access-date=19 October 2019|archive-date=19 October 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191019001913/https://www.gazeta-shqip.com/2014/01/21/tifozet-bojkot-ndaj-kosoves-nje-komb-nje-kombetare/|url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://telesport.al/tifozat-kuq-e-zi-nje-komb-nje-kombetare-zotohemi-te-ndodhe-sa-shpejt|title="Tifozat Kuqezi": Një komb, na duhet një kombëtare! Zotohemi të ndodhë sa më shpejt!|trans-title="Red and Black fans": One nation, we need one national team! We pledge to happen as soon as possible!|publisher=Telesport.al|language=sq|author=Alfred Lleshi|date=20 August 2016|access-date=19 October 2019|archive-date=19 October 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191019001910/https://telesport.al/tifozat-kuq-e-zi-nje-komb-nje-kombetare-zotohemi-te-ndodhe-sa-shpejt|url-status=dead}}</ref> In this sense, TKZ is joined by different supporters' associations throughout Albanian-speaking regions mainly in [[Kosovo]] (Shqiponjat of [[Peja (Kosovo)|Peja]], [[KF Vëllaznimi|Kuqezinjet e Jakoves]] of [[Gjakova]], [[FC Prishtina|Plisat]] of [[Pristina|Prishtina]], [[Torcida Mitrovicë|Torcida]] of [[Kosovska Mitrovica|Mitrovica]], etc.), [[North Macedonia]] ([[FK Shkëndija#Supporters|Ballistët]] of [[Tetovo|Tetovë]], [[FK Bashkimi (2011)|Ilirët]] of [[Kumanovo|Kumanovë]], [[FK Shkupi#Supporters|Shvercerat]] of [[Skopje|Shkupi]], etc.) and in Albania itself ([[FK Partizani Tirana#Ultras Guerrils|Ultras Guerrils]] of [[FK Partizani Tirana|Partizani Tirana]], [[Tirona Fanatics]] of [[KF Tirana]], [[KF Vllaznia Shkodër#Fans|Vllaznit Ultras]] of [[KF Vllaznia Shkodër|Vllaznia Shkodër]], [[KF Skënderbeu Korçë#Supporters|Ujqërit e Deborës]] of [[KF Skënderbeu Korçë|Skënderbeu Korçë]], Shqiponjat of [[KF Besa Pejë]] and many other different Albanian fans).<ref name="TKZ"/>

[[File:Tifosi albanesi stadio Palermo.jpg|thumb|[[Albanians|Albanian]] and [[Arbëreshë people|Arbëreshë]] fans in [[Palermo]], for the Italy-Albania [[2018 FIFA World Cup]] [[2018 FIFA World Cup qualification – UEFA Group G|qualifier match]] on 24 March 2017]]
The ongoing dispute between the Ministry of Culture, Youth and Sports and the Football Federation of Albania has been seen as a political intrusion by [[FIFA]] and [[UEFA]], which led to the banning of Albania from international sportive activities. FSHF president [[Armand Duka]] is highly unwanted by the TKZ who have numerously asked for his resignation believing he is responsible for internal corruption in the [[Albanian Football Federation]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://botapress.info/tifozet-kuq-e-zi-serish-kunder-dukes-foto/|title=Tifozët Kuq e Zi sërish kundër Dukës (Foto)|trans-title=Red and black fans again against Duka (Photo)|publisher=Botapress.info|language=sq|date=31 March 2016|access-date=19 October 2019|archive-date=19 October 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191019001914/http://botapress.info/tifozet-kuq-e-zi-serish-kunder-dukes-foto/|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.javanews.al/tifozet-kuq-e-zi-fyejne-rende-armand-duken/|title=Tifozët 'Kuq e Zi' fyejnë rëndë Armand Dukën|trans-title=Red and black fans heavily insult Armand Duka|publisher=Javanews.al|language=sq|date=13 November 2016|access-date=19 October 2019|archive-date=19 October 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191019001912/https://www.javanews.al/tifozet-kuq-e-zi-fyejne-rende-armand-duken/|url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.albaniasoccer.com/lajme/shqiperi/kombetarja/5579-ekskluzive--armand-duka-tifozat-kuq-e-zi-qfushateq-kunder-meje.html|title=Ekskluzive / Armand Duka: Tifozat kuq e zi, "fushate" kunder meje|trans-title=Exclusive/ Armand Duka: Red&black fans, a "campaign" against me|publisher=Albania Soccer|language=sq|date=9 November 2011|access-date=19 October 2019|archive-date=19 October 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191019001913/http://www.albaniasoccer.com/lajme/shqiperi/kombetarja/5579-ekskluzive--armand-duka-tifozat-kuq-e-zi-qfushateq-kunder-meje.html|url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.botasot.info/sporti/465418/tifozet-krenare-per-de-biasin-ovacione-kunder-dukes-video/|title=Tifozët krenarë për De Biasin…ovacione kundër Dukës (Video)|trans-title=The fans proud for De Biasi…ovations against Duka (Video)|publisher=Botasot.info|language=sq|date=12 October 2015|access-date=19 October 2019|archive-date=19 October 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191019001913/https://www.botasot.info/sporti/465418/tifozet-krenare-per-de-biasin-ovacione-kunder-dukes-video/|url-status=live}}</ref>

The TKZ have been praised by many different football players and managers, whom were not just Albanian. One example is with Switzerland's former coach, [[Ottmar Hitzfeld]], who was astonished by how many Albanian fans turned up and how enthusiastic they were in [[2014 FIFA World Cup qualification (UEFA)|2014 FIFA World Cup qualifying]] match between [[Switzerland national football team|Switzerland]] and Albania where the Swiss won 2–0 thanks to goals from [[Gökhan Inler]] and Kosovo-born [[Xherdan Shaqiri]]. He did not believe that there were 12,000 Albanian fans in the stands which was more than how many Swiss fans turned up for the game. He stated that "Albanian fans are fantastic and the most passionate fans I have ever seen".<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.botasot.info/sport-plus/186160/hitzfeld-tifozet-shqiptare-jane-fantastike/|title=Hitzfeld: Tifozët shqiptarë janë fantastikë|trans-title=Hitzfeld: Albanian fans are fantastic|publisher=Bota Sot|language=sq|date=12 September 2012|access-date=19 October 2019|archive-date=19 October 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191019001913/https://www.botasot.info/sport-plus/186160/hitzfeld-tifozet-shqiptare-jane-fantastike/|url-status=live}}</ref> During that campaign, TKZ attended all games Albania played apart from a match against [[Cyprus national football team|Cyprus]] in [[Nicosia]] and were also large in numbers in the away games to [[Slovenia national football team|Slovenia]] in [[Maribor]] and [[Norway national football team|Norway]] in Oslo.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://sport.albeu.com/kombetarja/slloveni-shqiperi-mbi-2000-tifoze-shqiptare-ne-lubjane/123142/|title=Slloveni-Shqipëri, mbi 2000 tifozë shqiptarë në Lubjanë|trans-title=Slovenia-Albania, over 2,000 Albanian fans in Ljubljana|publisher=Sport.albeu.com|language=sq|date=28 August 2013|access-date=19 October 2019|archive-date=19 October 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191019001911/http://sport.albeu.com/kombetarja/slloveni-shqiperi-mbi-2000-tifoze-shqiptare-ne-lubjane/123142/|url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://portalb.mk/76932-norvegji-shqiperi-3-mije-tifoze-kuqezi-ne-oslo/|title=Norvegji-Shqipëri, 3 mijë tifozë kuqezi në Oslo|trans-title=Norway-Albania, 3 thousand red&black fans in Oslo|publisher=Portalb.mk|language=sq|date=22 March 2013|access-date=19 October 2019|archive-date=19 October 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191019001914/https://portalb.mk/76932-norvegji-shqiperi-3-mije-tifoze-kuqezi-ne-oslo/|url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.panorama.com.al/sport/oslo-kuqezi-de-biazi-ja-si-i-mashtruam-norvegjezet/|title=Oslo kuqezi, De Biazi: Ja si i "mashtruam" norvegjezët…|trans-title=Oslo red&black, De Biasi: Here's how we "deceived" the Norwegians…|publisher=Panorama Sport|language=sq|author=Fation Shehu|location=Oslo|date=24 March 2013|access-date=19 October 2019|archive-date=19 October 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191019001909/http://www.panorama.com.al/sport/oslo-kuqezi-de-biazi-ja-si-i-mashtruam-norvegjezet/|url-status=live}}</ref>

==Results and fixtures==
{{main|Albania national football team results (2020–present)}}

The following is a list of match results in the last 12 months, as well as any future matches that have been scheduled.

{{legend2|#FFFFCC|Draw|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFCCCC|Loss|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFFFFF|Fixture|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}

|team1      = {{fb-rt|UKR}}
|goals1     = [[Oleksiy Hutsulyak|Hutsulyak]] {{goal|46}}
|team2      = {{fb|ALB}}
|goals2     = 
|stadium    = [[Estadio Ciutat de València]]{{efn|name=UKR|Due to the [[Russian invasion of Ukraine]], Ukraine are required to play their home matches at neutral venues until further notice.<ref name="ExCo">{{cite web|url=https://www.uefa.com/news-media/news/0272-148740475a92-1b97baa3e9d7-1000/|title=Decisions from today's extraordinary UEFA Executive Committee meeting|work=[[UEFA]]|publisher=Union of European Football Associations|date=25 February 2022|access-date=25 February 2022}}</ref>}}
|location   = [[Valencia]], Spain{{efn|name=UKR}}

|referee    = [[Juan Martínez Munuera]] ([[Royal Spanish Football Federation|Spain]])

}}
{{football box collapsible
|format     = 1
|date       = 3 June
|time       = 20:00
|round      = [[Exhibition game#Association football|Friendly]]
|score      = 0–1
|report     = https://eu-football.info/_match.php?id=19440
|team1      = {{fb-rt|ALB}}
|goals1     = 
|team2      = {{fb|ISR}}
|goals2     = [[Oscar Gloukh|Gloukh]] {{goal|73}}
|stadium    = [[Arena Kombëtare]]
|location   = [[Tirana]], Albania
|attendance = 6,600
|referee    = Miloš Savović ([[Football Association of Montenegro|Montenegro]])
|result     = L
}}

==Players==
{{See also|List of Albania international footballers}}
===Current squad===
*The following players were called up for the [[Exhibition game#Association football|friendly matches]] against [[Israel national football team|Israel]] and [[Luxembourg national football team|Luxembourg]], on 3 and 6 June 2026.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://fshf.org/sq/dy-risi-dhe-rikthime-ne-kombetare-trajneri-maran-publikon-listen-per-ndeshjet-ndaj-izraelit-luksemburgut/|title=Dy risi dhe rikthime në Kombëtare, trajneri Maran publikon listën për ndeshjet ndaj Izraelit & Luksemburgut|trans-title=Two new additions and returns to the national team, coach Maran publishes the list for the matches against Israel & Luxembourg|language=Albanian|publisher=[[Albanian Football Federation]]|date=28 May 2026}}</ref>
*''Caps and goals are correct {{as of|2026|6|6|lc=y}}, after the match against [[Luxembourg national football team|Luxembourg]].''<ref>{{cite web|url=https://eu-football.info/_match.php?id=19444|title=Albania vs Luxembourg, 6 June 2026|website=EU-Football.info|access-date=6 June 2026}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.uefa.com/european-qualifiers/friendlies/match/2048232--albania-vs-luxembourg/|title=Albania–Luxembourg|publisher=[[UEFA]]|access-date=6 June 2026}}</ref>
{{nat fs g start}}

{{nat fs break}}

{{nat fs g player|no=3|pos=DF|name=[[Mario Mitaj]]|age={{Birth date and age|2003|8|6|df=y}}|caps=33|goals=0|club=[[Al-Ittihad Club (Jeddah)|Al-Ittihad]]|clubnat=KSA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=4|pos=DF|name=[[Elseid Hysaj]]|other=[[Captain (association football)#Vice-captain|Captain]]|age={{Birth date and age|1994|2|2|df=y}}|caps=100|goals=2|club=[[SS Lazio|Lazio]]|clubnat=ITA}}

{{nat fs g player|no=6|pos=DF|name=[[Berat Djimsiti|Berat Gjimshiti]]|other=[[Captain (association football)#Vice-captain|Vice-captain]]|age={{Birth date and age|1993|2|19|df=y}}|caps=73|goals=1|club=[[Atalanta BC|Atalanta]]|clubnat=ITA}}

{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Jasir Asani]]|age={{Birth date and age|1995|5|19|df=y}}|caps=26|goals=6|club=[[Esteghlal F.C.|Esteghlal]]|clubnat=IRN|latest=v. {{fb|UKR}}, 31 March 2026}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=Florenc Farruku|age={{Birth date and age|2001|7|4|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[FC Dinamo City|Dinamo City]]|clubnat=ALB|latest=v. {{fb|POL}}, 26 March 2026<sup>TRA</sup>}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Rey Manaj]]|age={{Birth date and age|1997|2|24|df=y}}|caps=45|goals=11|club=[[Sivasspor]]|clubnat=TUR|latest=v. {{fb|ENG}}, 16 November 2025<sup>INJ</sup>}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Indrit Tuci]]|age={{Birth date and age|2000|9|14|df=y}}|caps=5|goals=0|club=[[Kayserispor]]|clubnat=TUR|latest=v. {{fb|ENG}}, 16 November 2025}}
{{nat fs break}}
;Notes
*<sup>INJ</sup> = It is not part of the [[#Current squad|current squad]] due to injury.
*<sup>TRA</sup> = Called up for training purposes only.
*<sup>PRE</sup> = Preliminary squad/standby.
<!--*<sup>CLUB</sup> = It is not part of the current squad after the club has not allowed him to join.
*<sup>COACH</sup> = It is not available in the next game due to suspended by the coach.
*<sup>SUS</sup> = It is not available in next match due to red card suspension.
*<sup>U21</sup> = Was called up from national [[Albania national under-21 football team|U21]] team.
*<sup>WD</sup> = Player withdrew from the current squad due to non-injury issue.--->
{{nat fs end}}

==Individual records==
{{main|Albania national football team records and statistics}}

{{See also|List of Albania international footballers|List of Albania national football team captains}}

:{{updated|6 June 2026}}<ref>{{cite web |title=Albania |url=https://www.national-football-teams.com/country/2/Albania.html |website=National Football Teams}}</ref>
:''Players in '''bold''' are still active with Albania.''

| style="text-align:left" | {{sortname|Altin|Rraklli}}
|11
|63
|{{#expr: 11/63 round 2}}
|1992–2005

| ||93 ||2002 ||9 ||92 ||align=left|{{increase}} 4 ||97 ||align=left|{{decrease}} 5
|-
| ||96 ||2001 ||7 ||74 ||align=left|{{increase}} 1 ||96 ||align=left|{{decrease}} 6
|-
| ||72 ||2000 ||8 ||72 ||align=left|{{increase}} 13 ||94 ||align=left|{{decrease}} 6
|-
| ||83 ||1999 ||8 ||83 ||align=left|{{increase}} 15 ||95 ||align=left|{{decrease}} 2
|-
| ||106 ||1998 ||8 ||97 ||align=left|{{increase}} 14 ||109 ||align=left|{{decrease}} 8
|-
| {{Color box|yellow|border=darkgray}} ||116 ||1997 ||7 ||113 ||align=left|{{increase}} 8 ||124 ||align=left|{{decrease}} 4
|-
| {{Color box|yellow|border=darkgray}} ||116 ||1996 ||5 ||90 ||align=left|{{increase}} 6 ||116 ||align=left|{{decrease}} 12
|-
| ||91 ||1995 ||8 ||87 ||align=left|{{increase}} 13 ||106 ||align=left|{{decrease}} 6
|-
| ||100 ||1994 ||5 ||93 ||align=left|{{increase}} 9 ||113 ||align=left|{{decrease}} 11
|-
| ||92 ||1993 ||7 ||91 ||align=left|{{increase}} 1 ||93 ||align=left|{{decrease}} 6
|-
| ||86 ||1992 ||  ||86 ||align=left|  ||86 ||align=left| 
|}

==Head-to-head record==
{{updated|6 June 2026 after the match against [[Luxembourg national football team|Luxembourg]].}}
{| bgcolor="#f7f8ff" cellpadding="3" cellspacing="0" border="1" style="font-size: 95%; border: grey solid 1px; border-collapse: collapse;text-align:center;"
|+'''Key'''
| style="background: #bbf3bb;"  width="20" |
| bgcolor="#ffffff" align="left" | '''Positive balance''' (more Wins)
|-
| style="background: #ffffbb;" width="20" |
| bgcolor="#ffffff" align="left" | '''Neutral balance''' (Wins = Losses)
|-
| style="background: #ffbbbb;" width="20" |
| bgcolor="#ffffff" align="left" | '''Negative balance''' (more Losses)
|}

{| class="wikitable sortable" style="text-align: center;"
|-
! style="width:200px"|Opponent
!{{Abbr|Pld|Played}}
!{{Abbr|W|Won}}
!{{Abbr|D|Drawn}}
!{{Abbr|L|Lost}}
!{{Abbr|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Abbr|GA|Goals against}}
!{{Abbr|GD|Goal difference}}
!{{Abbr|Win %|Winning percentage}}
!Reference

!69 nations!!409!!116!!85!!208!!393!!603!!−209!!28.3%!![https://eu-football.info/_matches.php?id=4&data=1 All H2H results]
|}
;Notes
{{notelist-ua}}

==Honours==
[[File:2007 stamps of Albania-National Team 1946.jpg|thumb|2007 Postage stamp of Albania, commemorating the 60th Anniversary of Victory at the Balkan Cup]]

===Regional===
* '''[[Balkan Cup]]'''
** '''Champions (1)''': [[1946 Balkan Cup|1946]]

===Friendly===
* '''[[Malta International Football Tournament|Malta International Tournament]]'''
** '''Champions (1)''': [[2000 Malta International Football Tournament|2000]]<ref>{{cite web|url=http://eu-football.info/_tournament.php?id=MIT-8|title=Rothmans Tournament 2000 match reports|access-date=19 June 2015|archive-date=13 May 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150513221330/http://eu-football.info/_tournament.php?id=MIT-8|url-status=live}}</ref>
